summary: Chlorhexidine mouthwash significantly decreases Streptococcus mutans levels within two weeks but its effectiveness diminishes after this period; however, it provides sustained protection lasting up to several months with regular usage. The optimal treatment duration appears to fall around an extended interval rather than strictly confined to only fourteen days as suggested by some earlier assessments due to lingering benefits observed clinically and supported by recent evidence. To ensure both immediate impact and long-term maintenance without unnecessary prolonged exposure or costs associated therewith, using chlorhexidine until at least one month may strike a balance
